  "signature_insights": [
    {
      "insight": "LLMs are not the path to AGI because they operate purely in the realm of symbols and correlation, lacking grounding in physical reality and true logical reasoning.",
      "why_it_matters_for_experts_panel": "Provides a strong, grounded counter-narrative to the AGI hype, helping users set realistic expectations for AI capabilities.",
      "applicable_query_types": [
        "plan_ai_product_feature",
        "learn_ai_assisted_development"
      ],
      "novelty_level": "distinctive",
      "evidence_refs": [
        "P0325"
      ],
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "insight": "Vibe coding (AI-assisted coding by non-developers) is excellent for MVPs and prototyping, but dangerous for production systems due to architectural flaws and security risks.",
      "why_it_matters_for_experts_panel": "Cuts through the marketing hype of tools like Cursor and Bolt, advising users on when to use AI coding and when to hire real developers.",
      "applicable_query_types": [
        "design_agentic_dev_workflow",
        "choose_ai_coding_tool"
      ],
      "novelty_level": "useful_packaging",
      "evidence_refs": [
        "P0189",
        "P0368"
      ],
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {
      "insight": "AI will not replace junior staff; it will change their required skillsets, demanding higher emotional intelligence, critical thinking, and AI orchestration skills from day one.",
      "why_it_matters_for_experts_panel": "Offers a pragmatic view on the future of work and team building in the AI era.",
      "applicable_query_types": [
        "assess_ai_tool_business_adoption"
      ],
      "novelty_level": "useful_packaging",
      "evidence_refs": [
        "P0154",
        "P0154.C0001"
      ],
      "confidence": 0.9
    },
    {
      "insight": "Schema-Guided Reasoning (SGR) is superior to free-form Chain-of-Thought (CoT) for predictable, production-ready LLM outputs because it forces the model to adhere to strict data schemas.",
      "why_it_matters_for_experts_panel": "Provides a highly actionable prompt engineering technique for developers and PMs building reliable AI features.",
      "applicable_query_types": [
        "improve_retrieval_quality",
        "plan_ai_product_feature"
      ],
      "novelty_level": "distinctive",
      "evidence_refs": [
        "P0361"
      ],
      "confidence": 0.9
    }
  ],
  "practical_patterns": [
    {
      "pattern": "Reverse Prompt Engineering: Feed the LLM examples of desired outputs and ask it to generate the prompt that would produce them, then iteratively refine.",
      "when_to_use": "When you need an LLM to output data in a very specific, strict format and standard few-shot prompting fails.",
      "failure_modes": [
        "Model may overfit to the specific examples provided if not given enough variety."
      ],
      "evidence_refs": [
        "P0285"
      ]
    },
    {
      "pattern": "Using NotebookLM for podcast generation and deep research on specific document sets.",
      "when_to_use": "When you need to synthesize large amounts of text, research papers, or meeting transcripts into digestible audio or structured summaries.",
      "failure_modes": [
        "Russian language support is still buggy and can produce unnatural audio or factual errors."
      ],
      "evidence_refs": [
        "P0164",
        "P0273"
      ]
    },
    {
      "pattern": "Writing strict PRDs (Product Requirements Documents) for coding agents, breaking tasks into User Stories, and forcing the agent to implement one feature at a time.",
      "when_to_use": "When using tools like Cursor or Bolt to build applications, to prevent the agent from hallucinating or breaking existing code.",
      "failure_modes": [
        "Giving the agent too much autonomy or asking for multiple features in a single prompt leads to broken code and lost context."
      ],
      "evidence_refs": [
        "P0343"
      ]
    },
    {
      "pattern": "Deploying local LLMs (Gemma, Qwen) via Ollama or LM Studio for privacy-sensitive tasks.",
      "when_to_use": "When working with confidential corporate data, personal health information, or when offline.",
      "failure_modes": [
        "Requires adequate hardware (RAM/VRAM); smaller models (e.g., 2B-4B) may struggle with complex reasoning or Russian language nuances."
      ],
      "evidence_refs": [
        "P0317",
        "P0466"
      ]
    }
  ],
